title: Maple Cricket League — a streetwear cricket league: vectorize the logo system, build the sublimation/screen-print kit artwork, and grade the brand photography into a production-ready package
one_line_ask: Turn Maple Cricket League's already-designed raster logo sketches and brand photos into a production-ready package: clean vector marks, a halftone sublimation/screen-print kit treatment, cohesive streetwear color-grade, a cut-out product shot, a wide banner reframed from the graded hero, and an exported, packaged deliverable set.

issues: Slight luminance noise/paper grain present but acceptable per brief
Ink texture is marginally uneven in places — acceptable and intentional per brief
Scan shadow faintly visible at edges — acceptable per brief
MCL monogram letterforms are slightly ambiguous in stacking order but legible
Small hat badge (rightmost mark) is slightly small relative to other marks but composition reads clearly
Panel is slightly rotated (~3 degrees) and off-center, but this is per brief and not a flaw.
Mild fabric texture and slight color cast present, as expected for a source asset.
Soft edges and minor luminance noise visible, but within acceptable range for a starting file.
Crossed bats are stylized/simplified and may read as generic batons rather than clearly cricket bats to a strict eye
Maple leaf silhouette is somewhat angular/stylized but acceptable for a crest
Slight softness at badge edges consistent with fabric texture — acceptable per brief
Panel rotation appears approximately 3 degrees — correct per brief
No significant AI artifacts detected
No real-world trademarks visible
Very slight softness on lower fold edges — within acceptable source-file tolerance
Contact shadow is minimal but present and acceptable
Fabric texture is convincingly photographic though borderline clean for a source file — not a CGI concern
Wicket is improvised (milk crate + stick) which reads authentically but may not immediately read as 'cricket' to all viewers — acceptable for documentary style
Slight motion blur on bat is present and appropriate
Light is dusk/mixed as briefed — flat and cool, no grading applied, correct
No CGI gloss detected — skin texture, pores, fabric wrinkles all read as genuine photographic capture
Composition is strong with batter as clear sharp foreground subject, fielders naturally placed in background
One fielder in background (dark shirt) is slightly obscured/dark but does not detract from primary subject
Chain-link fence and brick background present as briefed
Streetwear fits match brief (oversized tees, caps, track pants, gold chain)
South Asian cast reads authentically for GTA/Toronto laneway context
No severe AI artifacts detected — fingers, faces, hands all appear anatomically correct
No real-world brand logos or trademarks visible

assets.name: logo_system_master.ai
assets.note: Client-provided .ai template; represented here as an authored text proxy (binary Adobe format not synthesizable).
